test.detail: All key M3 test files pass: test_activation_quant.py (ALL TESTS PASSED, 9/9), test_w8a8_gemv.py (ALL TESTS PASSED, 8/8), test_w8a8_gemm.py (ALL TESTS PASSED, 11/11), test_w4a8_gemv.py (ALL TESTS PASSED), test_w4a8_repack.py (ALL TESTS PASSED, 7/7), test_flashattn_tune.py (ALL PASSED: 7 decode + 6 prefill correctness + benchmarks), test_engine_w8a8_w4a8.py (10/10 passed). Non-regression: test_gemm_fp16.py (PASS), test_elementwise.py (PASS), test_flash_attn_256.py (ALL TESTS PASSED), test_m1_integration.py (12/12 PASS). Pre-existing failure: test_m2_prefill.py has 1/10 failure (test_prefill_correctness_typical_shapes for seq=1 vs decode comparison) — predates M3 changes, documented in AGENTS.md Known Pre-Existing Issues.

appliedUpdates.description: Added W8A8/W4A8 kernel knowledge to architecture.md: Activation Quantization section (two-kernel pipeline, atomicMax trick, D2H sync limitation), W8A8 GEMV/GEMM section (performance characteristics, decode vs prefill tradeoffs, K alignment requirement), Engine Quantization Format Selection section (quant_format parameter, hard-error init, weight loader formats, D2H sync limitation).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add to Known Pre-Existing Issues: 'test_m2_prefill.py::test_prefill_correctness_typical_shapes fails with prefill(seq=1) vs decode max_err=1.619e-02 > 0.001. Different code paths between prefill(seq=1) and decode() produce slightly different outputs. Do not fix unless explicitly assigned.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature m3-engine-integration worker noted this pre-existing failure (9/10 tests pass in test_m2_prefill.py). The failure predates M3.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add to known guidance: 'W8A8 kernels are bandwidth-bound for decode (M=1) — they read 2x the weight bytes vs W4A16, making W4A16 1.8-2.4x faster for decode. Use W8A8 for prefill (M>1) batch scenarios. For decode, W4A16 v3_t16 remains the recommended default.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Benchmarks from m3-w8a8-perf and m3-engine-integration clearly show W8A8 decode performance gap. Architecture tradeoff is well understood.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: In kernel-optimization-worker skill, add note: 'When kernel signature takes scale_a by-value (not pointer), a 4-byte D2H sync is unavoidable when the scale lives on GPU. Document this as a known limitation and suggest the pointer-based alternative in discoveredIssues.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both m3-w8a8-gemv and m3-engine-integration workers noted the D2H sync limitation for scale_a. This pattern will recur for any future quantized kernels.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add to known guidance on SSH connectivity: 'When SSH_AUTH_SOCK is needed for ProxyJump, also remove stale known_hosts entries for 192.168.1.189 with ssh-keygen -R 192.168.1.189 when host key verification fails.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Current session encountered host key mismatch for 192.168.1.189 requiring manual removal. This will likely recur when LXC containers are reinstantiated.

rejectedObservations.observation: Suggest modifying W8A8/W4A8 kernel signatures to accept scale_a as device pointer instead of by-value
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a code change, not a library/services update. Excluded from library; included in suggestedGuidanceUpdates for future worker awareness.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add W8A8 GEMM K alignment runtime assertion
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a code change (adding assert K%16==0). Not a library entry. All Qwen shapes satisfy K%16==0. Low priority.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add kv_len < 4 edge case test for flash_attn_256_tuned decode kernel
rejectedObservations.reason: Test gap is non-blocking. Could be a future test enhancement but not a blocking review finding.

featureReviews.keyFinding: DPP 6-step butterfly + IEEE 754 atomicMax trick. 9/9 tests pass. Round-trip error within INT8 noise budget. Satisfies VAL-W8A8-003.
INT8 dot product intrinsic (sdot4) confirmed in source. fp16_rel < 0.3% for N=4096/11008 (well within 5%). INT32 accumulation correct. Performance: 78-139us for Qwen shapes. Satisfies VAL-W8A8-001.
64x64 tiles, TILE_K=16, INT32 accumulation. 11/11 test cases pass including tile boundaries and K alignment. 12.1 TFLOPS for 4096^3. Satisfies VAL-W8A8-002.
W4A16 is 1.82-2.42x faster for decode (bandwidth-limited, 2x weight bytes). W8A8 achieves 229-392 GB/s bandwidth utilization. Performance well-characterized. Satisfies VAL-W8A8-004.
Primary kernel uses sdot4 (INT8 dot product). max_rel_err < 0.05% for all shapes. GPTQ repacking exact. W4A8 is 1.12x faster than W4A16 for N=11008. Satisfies VAL-W4A8-001/002/003.

3.5-5.8x decode speedup via 4-wavefront KV-parallel split. 7 decode tests pass (max_err=0.000000, exact). 6 prefill tests pass (max_err < 1.3e-4). Original kernel non-regression confirmed. Satisfies VAL-FA-001/002.

quant_format='w4a16'/'w8a8'/'w4a8' with hard-error init. 10/10 engine tests pass. M1 (12/12) and M2 decode (8/8) non-regression confirmed. Weight loader correct for both W8A8/W4A8 formats. Satisfies VAL-ENG-001/002.

reviewNote: scrutiny-feature-reviewer subagents failed due to model configuration error (custom:CCS-sonnet not available). Reviews were conducted inline by the scrutiny-validator reading source code, handoff files, and live test output. This matches the pattern from the prior milestone (core-kernel-optimization).
